mainEntity.name: Can I be fired in Utah if I'm out of work for a neck injury?

acceptedAnswer.text: You cannot be fired in retaliation for filing a claim. However, Utah is an at-will state, meaning an employer can fire you for other reasons. Your workers' comp benefits should continue even if you are terminated.
acceptedAnswer.description:
Under Utah and Idaho law, your employer is prohibited from firing you *because* you filed a workers' compensation claim. This is called 'retaliatory discharge.' However, they *can* fire you for other reasons (like downsizing or performance) while you have an open claim. If you are fired, your medical benefits and lost wage checks (TTD) should continue as long as you are still disabled.

Key factors include the timing of the firing and the 'reason' given by the employer.

If you are fired right after reporting a neck injury, keep all your emails and texts. This could be the basis for an additional lawsuit for wrongful termination.

We protect our clients from employer bullying and ensure that being 'let go' doesn't mean your workers' comp benefits stop.
